codeReview.summary: The display-color-formatting feature was implemented by adding comprehensive integration tests to validate color control mechanisms. The existing output.rs implementation already correctly handled NO_COLOR env var, --no-color flag, and non-TTY auto-detection. The worker added 17 integration tests covering all three VAL-FMT assertions across tree, show, add, and init commands.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md documents that color output respects NO_COLOR env var and --no-color flag, and the output.rs implementation correctly follows this pattern using owo-colors with manual TTY detection via std::io::IsTerminal.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: werk-cli/src/output.rs:33-38 - uses std::env::var("NO_COLOR").is_err() and io::stdout().is_terminal() for color control

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-cli-worker skill procedure was followed correctly. Worker verified preconditions (display-tree and display-show-dynamics complete), wrote failing tests first (though existing impl was already correct), and ran all validators.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, handoff.verification.commandsRun shows all 4 validation commands passed

sharedStateObservations.observation: The has_ansi_codes helper in color.rs checks for both \x1b[ and \u{1b}[ escape sequences. This is a useful pattern for ANSI detection that could be documented in .factory/library/ for future test writers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: werk-cli/tests/color.rs:15-17 - helper function for ANSI detection

summary: PASS: The display-color-formatting feature is fully implemented and tested. The existing output.rs code already correctly implemented all three color control mechanisms (NO_COLOR=1, --no-color flag, non-TTY auto-detection). The worker added 17 comprehensive integration tests that verify: (1) VAL-FMT-001: NO_COLOR env var disables ANSI codes across all display commands, (2) VAL-FMT-002: --no-color flag disables colors, (3) VAL-FMT-003: non-TTY auto-detection works correctly. All tests pass, clippy is clean, and no sd-core regressions were introduced. The test file includes additional validation that output remains readable without colors and that JSON output never contains ANSI codes.
